skill.name: zoom-out
skill.description: Ask an AI coding agent to explain unfamiliar code from a higher-level perspective, including relevant modules, callers, and domain vocabulary.
skill.summary: Get a higher-level map of unfamiliar code before making changes.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3, but this is a false positive. The line contains only descriptive text about understanding the bigger picture of code and no cryptographic operation, executable code, prompt injection, or risky capability.

content.value_statement: Developers often need context before editing code they do not know. This skill prompts Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to explain the surrounding modules, callers, and domain concepts first.
actual_capabilities: Asks the agent to move up one abstraction level before answering.
Requests a map of relevant modules and callers.
Encourages use of the project domain glossary vocabulary.
Helps users understand unfamiliar code before making changes.
Provides a reusable prompt for architectural orientation.
limitations: It does not inspect files by itself; the host agent must perform any repository analysis.
It does not execute code, run tests, or verify runtime behavior.
It depends on available project context and may miss hidden dependencies.
It is a short prompt skill, not a full documentation generator.

use_cases.title: Onboard to a New Repository
use_cases.description: Ask for a module map before editing a codebase you have not worked in before.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Refactor
use_cases.description: Understand callers, boundaries, and domain terms before planning a change.

use_cases.title: Review Complex Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Get context around changed areas so review comments are grounded in system design.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Orientation
prompt_templates.prompt: I do not know this area of code well. Zoom out and explain what this part of the project does.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you first open an unfamiliar file or folder.

prompt_templates.title: Module Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Zoom out from this file. Give me a map of the relevant modules, callers, and data flow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before changing behavior that may affect other modules.

prompt_templates.title: Domain Vocabulary
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this area using the project domain vocabulary. Include the main concepts, owners, and boundaries.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when names and business concepts are unclear.

prompt_templates.title: Change Planning Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Before proposing changes, zoom out. Identify affected modules, important callers, likely invariants, and questions I should answer first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before refactors, migrations, or risky bug fixes.

output_examples.input: Zoom out from the billing service before I change invoice status handling.
output_examples.output: A concise map of billing modules, status owners, important callers, and terms used by the project.

output_examples.input: I do not understand this authentication folder. Give me the higher-level context.
output_examples.output: An overview of authentication responsibilities, request flow, related middleware, and areas to inspect next.

output_examples.input: Before this refactor, explain which modules depend on this parser.
output_examples.output: A dependency-focused explanation of parser callers, expected inputs, outputs, and change risks.

best_practices: Use it before editing unfamiliar code, especially shared modules.
Ask follow-up questions about unclear boundaries or domain terms.
Pair the explanation with file reads, tests, or call graph inspection when accuracy matters.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the overview as proof that a change is safe.
Do not use it as a replacement for reading critical code paths.
Do not ask for broad context when you already need a precise implementation detail.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It prompts an AI coding agent to explain unfamiliar code from a higher-level perspective.

faq.question: Does it modify code?
faq.answer: No. It only provides a prompt that asks for context and explanation.

faq.question: Can it build a complete architecture diagram?
faq.answer: It can request a module map, but the result depends on what the agent can inspect.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before changing code in an area where you lack architectural or domain context.

faq.question: Does it introduce security risk?
faq.answer: No evidence of security risk was found. The skill contains only a short natural-language prompt.
